ISO IEC 42005 Control Item(s): AI system description, scope, context, intended purpose, users, stakeholders, and assessment planning.
rationale: ARISE inventory provides the system definition and context needed before an AI impact assessment can be performed.

ISO IEC 42005 Control Item(s): Impact assessment planning, impact identification, evaluation, mitigation, documentation, and monitoring.
rationale: ARISE risk management directly supports the AI impact assessment lifecycle.

ISO IEC 42005 Control Item(s): Legal, rights, safety, privacy, ethical, and societal impact considerations.
rationale: ARISE regulatory compliance maps to determining legal and rights-based obligations during AI impact assessment.

ISO IEC 42005 Control Item(s): Data quality, provenance, representativeness, affected populations, and data-related impacts.
rationale: ARISE dataset controls support the data-related components of impact assessment.

ISO IEC 42005 Control Item(s): Transparency, explainability, affected-party understanding, and contestability.
rationale: ARISE explainability maps to impact-assessment requirements addressing stakeholder understanding and contestability.

ISO IEC 42005 Control Item(s): Review, approval, mitigation verification, monitoring, and reassessment.
rationale: ARISE verification supports confirming that impact-assessment mitigations were completed and continue to operate.
